title: Acoustic Theory of Speech Production
significance: Foundation for modern speech production. Source-filter model still used in all synthesis!

title: The Motor Theory of Speech Perception
significance: Proposed perception uses motor simulation. Hugely influential theory!

title: A Tutorial on Hidden Markov Models
significance: Statistical framework that revolutionized speech recognition systems.

title: On linguistic aspects of translation
significance: Foundational work on translation and cross-linguistic communication.

title: Articulatory Phonology
significance: Dynamic gestural approach. Paradigm shift from static to coordinated patterns!

title: The cortical organization of speech processing
significance: Dual processing streams model for speech in the brain.

title: Phonetic and phonological representation of stop consonant voicing
significance: Structured view of [voice] feature to phonetic implementation mapping.

title: A Mathematical Theory of Communication
significance: Information theory foundation for all digital communication including speech.

title: The discrimination of speech sounds within and across phoneme boundaries
significance: Categorical perception discovery. Revolutionized speech perception research!

title: Deep Neural Networks for Acoustic Modeling
significance: Deep learning sparked the neural network revolution in speech tech.

title: Categorical perception: real or myth? Or a bad question.
significance: CP is an empirical phenomenon and a theoretical claim about perceptual representations

title: Linear prediction: A tutorial review.
significance: An exposition of linear prediction in the analysis of discrete signals

title: Speech Perception in Infants
significance: Infants categorically perceive VOT distinctions. Early paper in the field of infant speech research

title: Hearing Lips and Seeing Voices
significance: Audiovisual illusion showed speech perception is multimodal

title: Cross-language Speech Perception: Evidence for Perceptual Reorganization During the First Year of Life
significance: Native language attunement shown in infants by 10-12 months. Critical period ideas

title: The TRACE Model of Speech Perception
significance: Interactive activation architecture for phoneme and word recognition. early tou computational model

title: Speech Errors as Linguistic Evidence
significance: Slips of the tongue reveal underlying phonological and lexical structure of language production

title: Functional parallelism in spoken word-recognition
significance: lexical access model - an early paper in word recognition research

title: Preliminaries to Speech Analysis
significance: Defined the distinctive feature system underpinning some modern phonology

title: The phonology and phonetics of English intonation
significance: This thesis develops a system of underlying representation for English intonation

title: On the quantal nature of speech
significance: Introduced Quantal Theory linking acoustic stability to articulatory regions. Explains why certain sounds are universally preferred.

title: A cross-language study of voicing in initial stops: Acoustical measurements
significance: Defined Voice Onset Time (VOT) as the primary acoustic cue for stop consonant voicing across languages.

title: Control methods used in a study of the vowels
significance: The classic acoustic study of American English vowels mapping F1 and F2 formants. Foundational acoustic data!

title: Human adults and human infants show a perceptual magnet effect for the prototypes of speech categories
significance: Demonstrated the perceptual magnet effect where acoustic space is warped around phonetic prototypes.

title: Statistical learning by 8-month-old infants
significance: Groundbreaking study showing infants use transitional probabilities to segment words from continuous speech.

title: Explaining phonetic variation: A sketch of the H&H theory
significance: Hypo- and Hyper-articulation theory. Speakers dynamically adapt their articulatory effort based on communicative needs.

title: Perceptual restoration of missing speech sounds
significance: Discovered the phonemic restoration illusion showing how the brain beautifully fills in obscured speech sounds.

title: Recognizing spoken words: The neighborhood activation model
significance: Introduced NAM demonstrating how the density of similar sounding words affects auditory word recognition.

title: Software for a cascade/parallel formant synthesizer
significance: Detailed the Klatt synthesizer architecture. An absolute cornerstone for early text-to-speech systems.

title: An event approach to the study of speech perception from a direct-realist perspective
significance: Argued listeners perceive actual articulatory events rather than abstract acoustic cues (Direct Realism).

title: Echoes of echoes? An episodic theory of lexical access
significance: Proposed that a speaker's specific voice details are stored with words, challenging abstract phonological representations.

title: The role of strong syllables in bootstrapping for lexical access
significance: Proposed the Metrical Segmentation Strategy showing how stress patterns help English listeners find word boundaries.

title: Infants' detection of the sound patterns of words in fluent speech
significance: Demonstrated that 7.5-month-old infants can segment and remember discrete words from running speech.

title: The listener as a source of sound change
significance: Seminal paper explaining how historical linguistic sound change originates directly from listener misperception.

title: The frame/content theory of evolution of speech production
significance: Linked rhythmic jaw cycles (frames) to the evolution and acquisition of universal syllable structures.

title: The Sound Pattern of English
significance: The definitive text of generative phonology establishing underlying rules and distinctive features.

title: Towards a neural basis of auditory sentence processing
significance: A highly influential neurocognitive model of speech comprehension mapping syntactic and semantic processing stages.

title: Shortlist: a connectionist model of continuous speech recognition
significance: Introduced the Shortlist model addressing how listeners segment continuous speech via lexical competition.

title: A direct realist view of cross-language speech perception
significance: Introduced the Perceptual Assimilation Model (PAM) explaining how non-native sounds are mapped to native categories.

title: Vocal tract length and formant frequency dispersion correlated with body size in rhesus macaques
significance: Crucial acoustic paper demonstrating how formant dispersion provides reliable cues to body size across species.
